title: Scratch Basics for Beginners
description: Step-by-step guide to building your first Scratch project — sprites, loops, and simple animations.

title: Python Fundamentals Handbook

title: Web Development Intro
description: HTML & CSS basics — build your first webpage in a single session. Includes student activity sheets.

title: Algorithm Thinking Workshop
description: Recording of the Fall 2025 Saturday training session covering sorting, searching, and algorithm design.

title: Robotics Activity Pack
description: Printable activity sheets for Raspberry Pi and LEGO robotics sessions — wiring guides, code snippets, and project challenges.

title: AI Ethics Discussion Guide
description: Classroom discussion prompts covering bias, privacy, and responsible AI. Designed for grades 5–8.

title: CS Unplugged Activities
description: Screen-free card activities for binary numbers, sorting algorithms, and logic puzzles. No devices required.

description: Everything a new volunteer needs to run their first session — lesson structure, classroom tips, and how to handle common challenges.

title: Scratch Game Design Challenge
description: Four-week project guide: design and share a fully playable Scratch game with levels, scoring, and original artwork.

title: Intro to Data Science
description: Using Python and simple datasets to spot real-world patterns. Includes a mini-project using El Paso weather data.

title: Parent & School Safety Guide
description: Overview of CodeCore's digital safety and responsible technology use policies — suitable to share with parents and administrators.

title: Coding Competition Prep
description: Practice problems and strategies for upcoming STEM fair competitions. Covers time management and presentation tips.

title: CodeCombat
description: Medieval-style online game where you control characters by typing real code. Great for young people and beginner adults.

title: Learn Code with Python — boot.dev
description: Interactive, game-like Python course that teaches coding through hands-on challenges and projects.

title: Codedex
description: Fun and beginner-friendly platform to learn how to code through gamified lessons and quests.

title: Coding for Kids — Python Courses
description: Kid-friendly Python courses designed to introduce programming concepts through guided exercises.

title: Flexbox Froggy
description: Interactive puzzle game that teaches CSS Flexbox by guiding frogs to lily pads.

title: CSS Grid Garden
description: Learn CSS Grid Layout fundamentals through fun, visual gardening challenges.

title: Learn Git Branching
description: Interactive visual game that teaches Git branching, merging, rebasing, and version control concepts.

title: Minecraft Adventurer — Code.org
description: Teaches core CS concepts — commands, repeat loops, and if statements — through a Minecraft-themed coding workspace.

title: Scratch — Imagine, Program, Share
description: Free block-coding website where students can create games, animations, and interactive stories.

title: Blockly Games: Maze
description: Maze game that teaches sequencing, loops, and conditionals through visual block coding.

title: Blockly Games
description: Collection of puzzle-style coding games that teach programming logic using drag-and-drop blocks.

title: Code.org Sprite Lab
description: Students create their own games and animations using drag-and-drop coding blocks. Great for grades 3–5.

title: Start Coding with Java in 10 Minutes
description: Short, beginner-friendly Java tutorial that covers core concepts like variables, loops, and methods.

title: Understanding Data Types in Programming
description: Clear explanation of data types — integers, strings, booleans, and more — for absolute beginners.

title: Intro to Programming: A Beginner's Guide
description: Overview of fundamental programming concepts for anyone just starting their coding journey.

title: Coding for Kids Explained — What & Why
description: Kid-friendly breakdown of what coding is, why it matters, and how it impacts everyday life.

title: Introduction to Coding for Young Kids
description: Age-appropriate introduction to coding concepts designed for elementary-age learners.

title: Programming Games with Solar 2D
description: YouTube series showing how to build games using the Solar 2D game engine and Lua scripting.

title: Programming Games with Lua
description: Video guide to building games using Lua, the scripting language behind Solar 2D and Roblox.

title: Real-Life Algorithms: Paper Airplanes
description: Code.org unplugged activity that explains algorithms using paper airplanes — no screen required.

title: The Odin Project — Foundations
description: Hands-on introduction to all the essential tools needed to build real, working websites — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Git.

description: In-depth introduction to JavaScript and full-stack web development, building on the Foundations path.

title: GeeksForGeeks
description: Comprehensive library of tutorials, coding problems, and explanations across all major computer science topics.

title: Android Developers — Get Started
description: Official Android development guides and video tutorials for building your first Android app.

title: Visual Studio Code
description: Free, lightweight code editor from Microsoft — the most popular editor for web and Python development.

title: Git — Windows Installer
description: Official Git installer for Windows. Required for version control and working with GitHub repositories.

title: Android Studio
description: Official IDE for Android development. Includes an emulator, debugger, and Kotlin/Java toolchain.

title: Solar2D — 2D Game Engine
description: Free, open-source 2D game engine using the Lua scripting language. Great for building mobile games.
